allowed_use: Show visible land use, approximate footprint, roads, buildings, substations, cooling yards, or construction change if date and source are recorded.
blocked_use: Do not infer GPU count, server count, power draw, energization, occupancy, customer workload, model training, emissions, or operational status.
required_evidence_before_final: Date-stamped image source, license, geolocation method, and independent source for any non-visible claim.

allowed_use: Use high/medium/low confidence labels and explain what anchors the location: official address, parcel, permit, utility page, local record, or map cross-check.
blocked_use: Do not present a visually plausible site as confirmed without authoritative location evidence.
required_evidence_before_final: Official address/parcel/permit or two independent location anchors with source dates.

allowed_use: Use visible substations, generators, batteries, cooling equipment, or transmission lines as physical context.
blocked_use: Do not infer MW, fuel mix, runtime, grid interconnection status, emissions, backup role, or site owner from imagery alone.
required_evidence_before_final: Permit, utility filing, interconnection document, environmental filing, or operator statement.

allowed_use: Use company announcements to identify named projects and the claims the company made at the time.
blocked_use: Do not treat announced capacity, chip counts, timelines, "world's most powerful" language, or partnership claims as delivered history without later same-scope evidence.
required_evidence_before_final: Official project update, regulatory filing, utility document, or independent reporting with date-bounded status.

allowed_use: Use OSM, USGS/Landsat, NASA, DOE, or other public/CC sources only with row-level attribution and license notes; use commercial satellite only with explicit license.
blocked_use: Do not screenshot Google Maps/Earth, Bing, Maxar, Apple Maps, or other commercial imagery for publication without rights clearance.
required_evidence_before_final: License text, attribution line, permitted-use note, and screenshot/source hash.

allowed_use: Use local-source surfaces to show that infrastructure raised public, utility, or permitting questions.
blocked_use: Do not claim health impacts, legal violations, emissions totals, local fuel mix, or community outcomes from imagery or advocacy snippets alone.
required_evidence_before_final: Official permit/regulatory source plus credible local/independent reporting, with exact date and scope.
